ruleset_version: Regelverk 2 - Operativt verifieringsregelverk för Certificate of Origin

certificate.label: Document A
certificate.quality_notes: Clear text-based PDF, fully legible. Certificate number SEG-23D-822427, ORIGINAL (1/1).

invoice.label: Document B
invoice.quality_notes: Clear text-based PDF, fully legible. Invoice number SI309039 from Bakels Sweden AB.

lc_check.lc_verification_reasoning: No Letter of Credit reference found in the certificate. The certificate number SEG-23D-822427 contains no LC-related terminology. No field labeled 'L/C', 'Letter of Credit', 'Documentary Credit' or similar exists in the certificate.

locations_found: Invoice footer address block: 'Bakels Sweden AB, von Utfallsgatan 26-30, SE-40252 Gothenburg, Sweden'
Invoice header branding: 'BAKELS - BAKERY INGREDIENTS SINCE 1904'
Invoice header address block: 'Bakels Sweden AB, von Utfallsgatan 26-30, SE-40252 Gothenburg, Sweden'

pairing_validation.reasoning: Criterion 2 is met: Certificate consignor 'Bakels Sweden AB' is identifiable in the invoice as the issuer (header, footer address block). Note: Certificate references invoice SI309081 but invoice number is SI309039 — this is a MISMATCH on invoice reference, but pairing validation passes because consignor is identifiable. Full verification proceeds.

candidates.raw_text: 692023 Cake Flavour K2000 Lemon in a package of not less than 200 kg Net weight: 17,760.00 Quantity: 17,760.00 Hs no:21069092 Origin: SWE
candidates.normalized_text: cake flavour k2000 lemon in a package of not less than 200 kg

candidates.raw_text: 225715 Jilk 60 Net weight: 2,048.00 Quantity: 2,048.00 Hs no:21069098 Origin: SWE
We hereby certify that this invoice is true and correct and that the goods are of Swedish origin.

16 Cake Flavour K2000 Lemon

candidates.normalized_text: 16 cake flavour k2000 lemon 4 pallets jilk 60

checkpoint_results.certificate_value: Bakels Sweden AB, Box 13099, 40252, Gothenburg, Sverige
checkpoint_results.invoice_value: Bakels Sweden AB, von Utfallsgatan 26-30, SE-40252 Gothenburg, Sweden

comparison_steps.description: Search for certificate consignor name in all invoice pages
comparison_steps.reasoning: Certificate consignor 'Bakels Sweden AB' is found explicitly in the invoice: (1) in the footer address block as 'Bakels Sweden AB', (2) in the header branding as 'BAKELS', and (3) as the invoice issuer. Exact name match after normalization. Country: Certificate says 'Sverige' (Sweden), invoice footer says 'Sweden', and VAT number prefix 'SE' confirms Sweden. MATCH via 4.1.0.

rules_applied.rule_description: Koncernstruktur – consignor name found explicitly in invoice
Land verification via VAT prefix SE confirms Sweden

normalization_applied: Company designation normalization: AB recognized as standard Swedish legal form
checkpoint_results.debug_explanation: Certificate consignor: 'Bakels Sweden AB' in Sweden (from 'Sverige'). Invoice issuer: 'Bakels Sweden AB' at 'von Utfallsgatan 26-30, SE-40252 Gothenburg, Sweden'. Name is exactly identical. Country verified via explicit 'Sweden' in address and VAT prefix 'SE' in SE556042136301. Address differs (Box 13099 vs von Utfallsgatan 26-30) but per rule 4.1.5 address differences do not cause MISMATCH. MATCH.

checkpoint_results.certificate_value: CBL Foods International (PVT) Ltd, P.O. Box 1, Habarakada Road, RANALA, SRI LANKA
checkpoint_results.invoice_value: National Food Company, Cake Division (AMERICANA), SA21543 Jeddah, Saudi Arabia

comparison_steps.description: Identify the primary consignee/buyer in the invoice
comparison_steps.result: National Food Company identified as buyer/business address party
comparison_steps.reasoning: The invoice has 'Business address' and 'Delivery address' fields both showing 'National Food Company, Cake Division (AMERICANA), SA21543 Jeddah, Saudi Arabia'. There is no separate 'Invoice to' / 'Bill to' / 'Sold to' / 'Buyer' field. The business address is the closest equivalent to a billing party. The invoice issuer is Bakels Sweden AB. The buyer/consignee is National Food Company.

comparison_steps.step: 4.2.0 – Compare certificate consignee to invoice buyer
comparison_steps.description: Compare 'CBL Foods International (PVT) Ltd' against 'National Food Company'
comparison_steps.reasoning: Certificate consignee is 'CBL Foods International (PVT) Ltd' in Sri Lanka. Invoice buyer/delivery party is 'National Food Company' in Saudi Arabia. These are completely different company names in completely different countries. The name 'CBL Foods International' does not appear anywhere in the invoice.

comparison_steps.description: Search for 'CBL Foods International' in all fields of the invoice
comparison_steps.reasoning: Searched all text in the invoice including header, footer, address blocks, line items, summary section, and declaration text. The name 'CBL Foods International' does not appear anywhere in the invoice. No variant of 'CBL' appears either.

comparison_steps.description: Check if names can be reconciled via normalization rules 4.1.3.1-4.1.3.6
comparison_steps.reasoning: The names 'CBL Foods International' and 'National Food Company' are entirely different legal entities. No normalization rule can reconcile completely different company names. Rules 4.1.3.2 (layout truncation), 4.1.3.3 (commercial abbreviation), 4.1.3.4 (commercial vs registered name), 4.1.3.5 (omitted organization words), 4.1.3.6 (legal form abbreviation), 4.1.3.7 (missing words), 4.1.3.7.1 (division words) — none apply because the core identity-bearing names share no common elements.

comparison_steps.description: Compare countries: Sri Lanka vs Saudi Arabia
comparison_steps.reasoning: Certificate consignee country: Sri Lanka. Invoice buyer/delivery country: Saudi Arabia. Countries do not match.

rules_applied.rule_description: Priority order for consignee identification — business address used as closest billing equivalent
Search in all invoice fields for certificate consignee
MISMATCH when consignee cannot be identified in invoice

normalization_applied: Case normalization applied to company names
checkpoint_results.debug_explanation: Certificate consignee is 'CBL Foods International (PVT) Ltd' in Sri Lanka. The invoice is addressed to 'National Food Company, Cake Division (AMERICANA)' in Jeddah, Saudi Arabia. These are entirely different companies in different countries. 'CBL Foods International' does not appear anywhere in the invoice text. No normalization rule can bridge this gap — the names share no identity-bearing elements. Both company name and country are mismatched. MISMATCH per 4.2.4.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: CBL Foods International could be a parent company or agent of National Food Company
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: Rule 4.2 prohibits assumptions about group structures, agent relationships or trade relationships. No evidence in the invoice links these two entities.

Rule 4.2.0.3 requires that the certificate consignee can be identified in the invoice's Ship-To field. The invoice's delivery address shows 'National Food Company' in Saudi Arabia, not 'CBL Foods International' in Sri Lanka. The certificate consignee appears nowhere in the invoice.

checkpoint_results.invoice_value: Cake Flavour K2000 Lemon, Jilk 60 (Invoice SI309039)

comparison_steps.description: Check if certificate contains article numbers
comparison_steps.reasoning: Certificate does not contain article numbers. Proceeding to Priority 2.

comparison_steps.description: Identify the identity-bearing main designation in the certificate
comparison_steps.reasoning: The certificate goods description in box 6 states 'Bakery Ingredients' as the product description. The invoice describes products as 'Cake Flavour K2000 Lemon' and 'Jilk 60'. The term 'Bakery Ingredients' does not appear verbatim in the invoice line items.

comparison_steps.step: 4.3.5 Priority 3 – Generic goods description with invoice reference
comparison_steps.description: Check if generic description with valid invoice reference applies
comparison_steps.reasoning: Certificate contains generic description 'Bakery Ingredients' and references 'Invoice SI309081' in Remarks (box 5). However, the invoice in the document pair bears number SI309039, NOT SI309081. The invoice reference in the certificate does NOT match the invoice provided. Per rule 4.3.5, if the reference does not correspond → MISMATCH. Additionally, rule 8.5.2 states that if the certificate refers to a specific invoice number but the invoice file does not contain that number, the result should be MANUAL_REVIEW. However, the consignor IS identifiable (pairing validation passed via criterion 2), and we can proceed with verification — the invoice reference itself constitutes a MISMATCH on goods description since the referenced invoice is not the one provided.

comparison_steps.description: No valid invoice reference match to verify date against
comparison_steps.reasoning: Since the invoice number does not match, date verification under 4.3.5 is moot.

rules_applied.rule_description: Priority order: article numbers → text matching → generic with reference
rules_applied.outcome: No article numbers; text matching fails; generic reference fails

rules_applied.rule_description: Identity-bearing main designation 'Bakery Ingredients' not found verbatim in invoice
Generic goods description with invoice reference — reference SI309081 does not match invoice SI309039

checkpoint_results.debug_explanation: Certificate describes goods as 'Bakery Ingredients' (generic) and references 'Invoice SI309081'. The invoice in this document pair is number SI309039, not SI309081. The invoice reference in the certificate does not match the provided invoice. Under Priority 2, 'Bakery Ingredients' does not appear as a verbatim text string in the invoice line items (which say 'Cake Flavour K2000 Lemon' and 'Jilk 60'). The invoice header does show 'BAKERY INGREDIENTS SINCE 1904' as part of the Bakels branding, but this is a company slogan/tagline, not a goods description. Under Priority 3 (rule 4.3.5), the generic description could be accepted IF the invoice reference matches — but SI309081 ≠ SI309039. Therefore MISMATCH.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: 'Bakery Ingredients' is a general category that encompasses 'Cake Flavour K2000 Lemon' and 'Jilk 60'
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: Semantic interpretation and category matching are prohibited under rules 4.3.3 and 4.3. The identity-bearing main designation must be found verbatim. 'Bakery Ingredients' ≠ 'Cake Flavour K2000 Lemon' or 'Jilk 60'.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: The invoice header 'BAKERY INGREDIENTS SINCE 1904' confirms the goods description
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: This text is part of the company branding/slogan for Bakels, not a goods description in the invoice line items.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: SI309081 and SI309039 are close numbers from the same company, so they may refer to related shipments
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: Rule 4.3.5 requires EXACT match of invoice reference. SI309081 ≠ SI309039. No approximation or assumption of relatedness is permitted.

checkpoint_results.invoice_value: Net weight: 19 908 kg / Gross weight: 21 028 kg

comparison_steps.description: Box 7 contains: 9600 kg net and 10400 kg gross
comparison_steps.result: Two quantity values identified: NW 9600 kg, GW 10400 kg
comparison_steps.reasoning: Both net weight and gross weight are explicitly stated in box 7 with units and weight categories.

comparison_steps.step: 4.4.1 – Occurrence check: NW 9600 kg in invoice
comparison_steps.reasoning: Invoice states 'Net weight: 19 908 kg'. Value 9600 does not appear anywhere in the invoice. Per-line net weights are 1.00, 17,760.00, and 2,048.00 — none equal 9600.

comparison_steps.step: 4.4.1 – Occurrence check: GW 10400 kg in invoice
comparison_steps.reasoning: Invoice states 'Gross weight: 21 028 kg'. Value 10400 does not appear anywhere in the invoice.

comparison_steps.description: Check if invoice explicitly contradicts certificate quantities
comparison_steps.reasoning: Certificate NW: 9600 kg. Invoice NW: 19,908 kg. Both are explicit net weight values. Difference: 19908 vs 9600 = 107.4% difference, far exceeding ±0.1% tolerance. Certificate GW: 10400 kg. Invoice GW: 21,028 kg. Both are explicit gross weight values. Difference: 21028 vs 10400 = 102.2% difference, far exceeding ±0.1% tolerance. Both NW and GW values in the certificate are explicitly contradicted by the invoice.

comparison_steps.description: Check if any quantity exception applies
comparison_steps.reasoning: 3A (multiple invoices): Only one invoice. 3B (MT↔KG): Both documents use KG, no unit conversion needed. 3C (unit weight derivation): Certificate gives total, not per-unit. 3D (sum of lines within invoice): Sum of invoice net weights: 1.00 + 17,760.00 + 2,048.00 = 19,809.00 — this does not equal 9,600. 3E (sum of row weights when total missing): Invoice has explicit totals. No exception applies.

rules_applied.rule_description: Occurrence check — 9600 and 10400 not found in invoice
Explicit contradiction — invoice NW 19908 kg ≠ certificate NW 9600 kg; invoice GW 21028 kg ≠ certificate GW 10400 kg
MISMATCH — values do not match and are explicitly contradicted

checkpoint_results.debug_explanation: Certificate box 7: 9600 kg net, 10400 kg gross. Invoice total: Net weight 19,908 kg, Gross weight 21,028 kg. Neither the net nor gross weight in the certificate can be identified in the invoice. The invoice explicitly states different values for both NW and GW, constituting an explicit contradiction per rule 4.4.3.1. The difference is approximately double (the invoice shows roughly twice the certificate's values), consistent with this being a different shipment or a combined shipment. No exception (3A-3E) applies. MISMATCH.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: The certificate covers a partial shipment of the invoice
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: Rules prohibit proportional calculations or assumptions about partial shipments. The certificate quantities must be identifiable in the invoice per rule 4.4.1.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: Shipment quantity rule 4.4.3.2 could apply (20 pallets)
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: The certificate contains explicit weight values in box 7 (9600 kg net, 10400 kg gross). Per rule 4.4.2.1, box 7 values are the verification-grounding quantity. The shipment quantity '20 pallets' in box 6 is secondary. Even if 20 pallets were accepted, the explicit weight contradiction remains — the invoice states different NW and GW values that directly contradict the certificate.

checkpoint_results.invoice_value: Origin: SWE; 'goods are of Swedish origin'

comparison_steps.description: Search for explicit origin fields in invoice
comparison_steps.result: Found: 'Origin: SWE' on line items 2 and 3; 'goods are of Swedish origin' in declaration text
comparison_steps.reasoning: Invoice line items for Cake Flavour K2000 Lemon and Jilk 60 both state 'Origin: SWE'. The declaration text states 'the goods are of Swedish origin'. Line 1 (Freight) has blank origin, which is expected for a freight/service line.

comparison_steps.description: Normalize 'EC; SWEDEN' from certificate and 'SWE' / 'Swedish origin' from invoice
comparison_steps.result: Certificate: EC (European Community) + Sweden. Invoice: SWE = Sweden.
comparison_steps.reasoning: SWE is ISO alpha-3 code for Sweden. 'Swedish origin' = Sweden. EC = European Community = European Union.

comparison_steps.description: Certificate says 'EC; SWEDEN'. Invoice says 'SWE' (Sweden). Sweden is an EU member state.
comparison_steps.reasoning: Certificate origin: EC (European Community) and SWEDEN. Invoice origin: SWE (Sweden). Sweden is an EU member state. Per rule 4.5.4.2, EU ↔ specific EU member state is verifiable in both directions. Per rule 4.5.4.2.1, certificate states 'EC' combined with one specific EU member state 'SWEDEN'. Invoice confirms SWE (Sweden) as origin. The invoice also contains the general declaration 'goods are of Swedish origin'. No non-EU origins appear in the invoice. All invoice origin fields show SWE. MATCH.

comparison_steps.step: 4.5.4.1 – Check for non-EU origins in invoice
comparison_steps.description: Verify no non-EU origin countries appear in invoice
comparison_steps.reasoning: All goods-related origin fields in invoice show 'SWE' (Sweden, EU member). Freight line has blank origin. No non-EU origins present.

rules_applied.rule_description: Origin normalization: SWE → Sweden, EC → European Community/EU
EU ↔ specific EU member state equivalence
General EU origin statement covers 'EU [country]' combination
No non-EU origins in invoice — no contradiction

checkpoint_results.debug_explanation: Certificate origin field (box 3): 'EC; SWEDEN'. This means European Community and Sweden. Invoice origins: 'Origin: SWE' on both product lines, plus declaration 'goods are of Swedish origin'. SWE = Sweden, which is an EU member state. Per rule 4.5.4.2.1, when certificate says 'EU [country]' and invoice confirms that country's origin, MATCH. No non-EU origins appear in the invoice. MATCH.

evidence_records.raw_text: National Food Company, Cake Division (AMERICANA), SA21543 Jeddah, Saudi Arabia
CBL Foods International does not appear anywhere in invoice text
We hereby certify that this invoice is true and correct and that the goods are of Swedish origin.

rule_evaluation_log.reasoning: No Letter of Credit reference found in the certificate.
Document A contains one certificate (ORIGINAL). Document B contains one invoice. Standard single-document pairing.

rule_evaluation_log.rule_description: Invoice reference in certificate vs invoice file
rule_evaluation_log.reasoning: Certificate references invoice SI309081. Invoice file contains invoice SI309039. These do not match. However, consignor is identifiable (Bakels Sweden AB), so pairing validation passes via criterion 2. The invoice number mismatch is reflected in the goods description checkpoint.
Criterion 2 met: Certificate consignor 'Bakels Sweden AB' identifiable in invoice.
Consignor name found explicitly in invoice footer and header.
Invoice has Business address and Delivery address fields showing National Food Company.
Certificate consignee 'CBL Foods International (PVT) Ltd' does not appear in any invoice field, including Ship-To.

rule_evaluation_log.rule_description: Generic goods description with invoice reference
rule_evaluation_log.reasoning: Certificate references SI309081 but invoice is SI309039. Reference does not match.
Both NW and GW values differ substantially between certificate and invoice.
Certificate gives total weight, not per-unit weight.

rule_evaluation_log.rule_description: Exception 3D – Sum of lines within same invoice
Exception 3E – Sum of row weights when total missing

rule_evaluation_log.reasoning: EC + SWEDEN verified against SWE (Sweden) in invoice.

overall_assessment.human_explanation: Certifikatet och fakturan avser sannolikt olika försändelser. Certifikatet anger mottagaren som CBL Foods International (PVT) Ltd i Sri Lanka, men fakturan är ställd till National Food Company i Saudiarabien. Certifikatet refererar till fakturanummer SI309081 men den bifogade fakturan har nummer SI309039. Dessutom anger certifikatet nettovikt 9 600 kg och bruttovikt 10 400 kg, medan fakturan visar 19 908 kg netto och 21 028 kg brutto.

summary_of_mismatches.description: Certificate consignee 'CBL Foods International (PVT) Ltd' (Sri Lanka) cannot be identified in the invoice. Invoice buyer is 'National Food Company' (Saudi Arabia).
Certificate references invoice SI309081 but the provided invoice is SI309039. Generic goods description 'Bakery Ingredients' cannot be verified without matching invoice reference.
Certificate: 9600 kg NW / 10400 kg GW. Invoice: 19,908 kg NW / 21,028 kg GW. Explicit contradiction on both weight categories.

blocking_issues: Wrong invoice attached: certificate references SI309081, invoice is SI309039
Different consignee: CBL Foods International (Sri Lanka) vs National Food Company (Saudi Arabia)
Weight values do not match: approximately double in invoice vs certificate

human_readable_report.title: Verifieringsrapport – Certificate of Origin mot Faktura

sections.certificate_value: Bakels Sweden AB, Box 13099, 40252, Gothenburg, Sverige
sections.invoice_value: Bakels Sweden AB, von Utfallsgatan 26-30, SE-40252 Gothenburg, Sweden
sections.rule_applied: Konsignörens namn identifierat direkt i fakturan (4.1.0). Land verifierat via adressblock och VAT-prefix SE (4.1.2.1).
sections.motivation: Certifikatets avsändare 'Bakels Sweden AB' i Sverige är identisk med fakturans utställare 'Bakels Sweden AB' i Sweden. Adressskillnad (Box 13099 vs von Utfallsgatan 26-30) påverkar inte bedömningen per 4.1.5. Land bekräftat via explicit 'Sweden' och VAT-prefix SE.

sections.certificate_value: CBL Foods International (PVT) Ltd, P.O. Box 1, Habarakada Road, RANALA, SRI LANKA
sections.invoice_value: National Food Company, Cake Division (AMERICANA), SA21543 Jeddah, Saudi Arabia
sections.rule_applied: Mottagarens namn kan inte identifieras i fakturan (4.2.4). Helt olika företagsnamn och olika länder.
sections.motivation: Certifikatet anger mottagare 'CBL Foods International (PVT) Ltd' i Sri Lanka. Fakturan är ställd till 'National Food Company' i Jeddah, Saudiarabien. Namnen delar inga identitetsbärande element. 'CBL Foods International' förekommer inte någonstans i fakturatexten. Både företagsnamn och land avviker. MISMATCH.

sections.invoice_value: Cake Flavour K2000 Lemon, Jilk 60 (fakturanummer SI309039)
sections.rule_applied: Generell varubeskrivning med fakturareferens (4.3.5) – fakturareferensen i certifikatet (SI309081) överensstämmer inte med den bifogade fakturans nummer (SI309039).
sections.motivation: Certifikatet anger generell varubeskrivning 'Bakery Ingredients' och hänvisar till 'Invoice SI309081'. Den bifogade fakturan har nummer SI309039, vilket inte överensstämmer. Utan giltig fakturareferens kan den generella varubeskrivningen inte verifieras. 'Bakery Ingredients' förekommer inte som varubeskrivning i fakturans artikelrader. MISMATCH.

sections.invoice_value: Net weight: 19 908 kg / Gross weight: 21 028 kg
sections.rule_applied: Uttrycklig motsägelse (4.4.3.1) – både netto- och bruttovikt i certifikatet avviker väsentligt från fakturans värden.
sections.motivation: Certifikatet: 9 600 kg netto, 10 400 kg brutto. Fakturan: 19 908 kg netto, 21 028 kg brutto. Båda viktkategorierna (NW och GW) anges uttryckligen i båda dokumenten och avviker väsentligt (mer än 100% avvikelse). Detta utgör en uttrycklig motsägelse per 4.4.3.1. Inget undantag (3A–3E) är tillämpligt. MISMATCH.

sections.invoice_value: Origin: SWE; 'goods are of Swedish origin'
sections.rule_applied: EU-normalisering (4.5.4.2, 4.5.4.2.1) – EC + Sweden verifierat mot SWE i fakturans ursprungsfält.
sections.motivation: Certifikatet anger 'EC; SWEDEN' som ursprung. Fakturans artikelrader anger 'Origin: SWE' (= Sweden) och deklarationen bekräftar 'goods are of Swedish origin'. Sweden är EU-medlemsstat. Per 4.5.4.2.1 täcker fakturans bekräftelse av svenskt ursprung certifikatets angivelse 'EC; SWEDEN'. Inga icke-EU-ursprung förekommer i fakturan. MATCH.

output_quality_checks.consistency_details: Three checkpoints show MISMATCH (4.2, 4.3, 4.4). comparison_result is NOT_IDENTICAL. Consistent.
output_quality_checks.confidence_floor_details: All MATCH checkpoints have confidence ≥ 0.85. NOT_IDENTICAL result, so confidence floor for IDENTICAL is not the binding constraint.
